---
title: "AI in Health and Social Care"
subtitle: "Vision for MSc course"
author: "Milan Valášek"
date: "2 May 2022"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    lib_dir: libs
    css: slides.css
    nature:
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)
```

```{js, echo = FALSE}
window.onload = function() {
  const usher_logo = document.createElement("div");
  usher_logo.classList.add("logo");
  usher_logo.classList.add("inst-logo");
  usher_logo.innerHTML = "<img src='./usher_logo.svg'/>";
  const uoe_logo = document.createElement("div");
  uoe_logo.classList.add("logo");
  uoe_logo.innerHTML = "<img src='./uoe_logo.png'/>";
  title_slide = document.querySelector(".title-slide");
  title_slide.appendChild(usher_logo);
  title_slide.appendChild(uoe_logo);
};

```

## In this presentation

- Challenges

- Learning outcomes and prerequisites

- Course structure and content

- Assessment

---

## Challenges

- Diverse student population with varying degrees of computing/programming and data-analytical skills

--

- Provide a high-level understanding of a range of AI tools and techniques with hands-on experience, while covering topics specific to H&SC

---

## Learning outcomes

- Knowledge of a variety of contexts within H&SC where AI is and can be useful
- Ability to communicate with H&SC professionals and users as well as computer/data science experts about AI
- Hands-on experience of machine learning methods
- Appreciation of the challenges, limitations, and ethical and security issues associated with AI

--

## Prerequisites

- Understanding of issues in data curation in H&SC
  - Data ethics and ownership OR
  - Data standards and core technologies in health and social care
  
- Basic computing and statistics skills
  - Data Analysis with R
  - Data visualisation: knowledge transfer (?)
  
---

## Structure

- Series of short videos (max 15 min) OR standard lectures
- Interactive tutorials/worksheets
- Seminars / Live coding workshops
- Required and recommended reading
- Discussion topics
 
--

## Content

**Week 1:** Uses of AI in H&SC present and future

**Week 2:** What _actually_ is AI + case studies

**Week 3:** Overview of AI tools (NLP, machine learning, robotics)

**Week 4:** Selected ML methods in `R` (or Python) - practical experience

**Week 5:** Ethical issues and limitations of AI in H&SC

---

.pull-left[
### Learning outcomes

- Knowledge of a variety of contexts within H&SC where AI is and can be useful

- Ability to communicate with H&SC professionals and users as well as computer/data science experts about AI

- Hands-on experience of machine learning methods
]

.pull-right[
### Assessment

- 100% Coursework

- Discussion board engagement

- Reading journal

- Coursework assignment
  - Essay on a selection of topics (*e.g.*, Sources and consequences of bias in AI)
  - Coding assignment (training a simple ML model)
]

---

background-image: url("./hal.jpg")
background-size: cover


.joke[
I'm sorry panel

I'm afraid this is the end
]
